ingliz_tili.text: In which context is 'shall' used in modern formal English to express obligation?
options: The tenant shall maintain the property in good condition.
I shall never forget this day.
You shall go to the ball!
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Shall' huquqiy matnlarda majburiyat ifodalaydi.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence demonstrates the correct distinction between 'used to' and 'would' for past habits?
options: When I was a child, I would live in the countryside.
When I was a child, I used to live in the countryside.
When I was a child, I would have lived in the countryside.
When I was a child, I used to living in the countryside.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Used to' state va habit uchun, 'would' faqat action habit uchun.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'will' for a future prediction based on a personal opinion rather than evidence?
options: Look at those clouds – it will rain.
I think it will rain tomorrow.
The forecast says it will rain.
It will rain by the time we arrive.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'I think + will' – shaxsiy fikr, dalilsiz bashorat.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence shows correct subject-verb agreement with a collective noun in British English?
options: The government have announced their decision.
The government has announced their decision.
The government have announced its decision.
The government has announced its decision.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Britaniya ingliz tilida kollektiv ot ko'plik (individual a'zolar).

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence where 'in case' is used correctly with a past reference.
options: I took an umbrella in case it rains.
I took an umbrella in case it rained.
I took an umbrella in case it will rain.
I took an umbrella in case it would rain.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Pastda 'in case' + past simple.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly distinguishes 'despite' and 'although' with a clause?
options: Despite he was tired, he finished the work.
Despite being tired, he finished the work.
Although being tired, he finished the work.
Despite of being tired, he finished the work.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Despite + gerund', 'although + clause'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'neither...nor' correctly with three elements?
options: Neither John, Mary, nor Peter came.
Neither John nor Mary nor Peter came.
Neither John, nor Mary, nor Peter came.
Neither John or Mary or Peter came.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Neither A nor B nor C' – takrorlanuvchi 'nor'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'so' and 'such' with a countable noun?
options: It was so beautiful day that we stayed outside.
It was such beautiful day that we stayed outside.
It was so a beautiful day that we stayed outside.
It was such a beautiful day that we stayed outside.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Such a/an + adjective + singular countable noun'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct reported speech transformation of 'I will help you tomorrow.'
options: He said he will help me tomorrow.
He said he would help me the next day.
He said he would help me tomorrow.
He said he will help me the next day.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Will → would', 'tomorrow → the next day'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'may' appropriately in a formal written request?
options: You may leave if you want.
Students may use the library after 6 PM.
May you help me with this?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'May' ruxsat uchun – rasmiy yozma qoidalarda.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'can' to express a general possibility?
options: It can get very cold here in winter.
You can leave now if you wish.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Can' umumiy ehtimollik (general possibility).

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence where 'need' is used as a modal verb in a negative question.
options: Need you make so much noise?
Do you need to make so much noise?
Needn't you make so much noise?
Don't you need to make so much noise?
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Need you?' – modal 'need' (without 'to') in questions.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'dare' as a semi-modal in a negative statement?
options: I dare not speak the truth.
I don't dare speak the truth.
I dare not to speak the truth.
I don't dare to speak the truth.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Dare not + infinitive without to' – semi-modal usage.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence demonstrates the correct use of 'used to' to contrast past and present?
options: I used to smoke, but I don't anymore.
I used to smoke, but I wouldn't anymore.
I used to smoke, but I'm not anymore.
I used to smoke, but I didn't anymore.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Used to' past habit vs present contrast.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence where 'would' expresses a typical past behavior with irritation.
options: He would always forget his keys.
He used to always forget his keys.
He would have forgotten his keys.
He would be forgetting his keys.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Would + always' – typical past behavior (sometimes irritation).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'be to' for a formal future arrangement?
options: The president is to visit France next week.
The president will visit France next week.
The president is going to visit France next week.
The president visits France next week.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Be to + infinitive' – rasmiy kelishuv, reja.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'be about to' correctly for an immediate future?
options: I am about to leave when the phone rang.
I was about to leave when the phone rang.
I am about to have left when the phone rang.
I was about to have left when the phone rang.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Was about to' + past event interruption.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence where 'will have + past participle' is used correctly for a future perfect prediction.
options: By next year, I will have finished my degree.
By next year, I will finish my degree.
By next year, I have finished my degree.
By next year, I will be finishing my degree.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Will have + V3' – future perfect.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'would have + past participle' for a past unreal conditional?
options: If I knew, I would have helped.
If I had known, I would have helped.
If I would have known, I helped.
If I knew, I would help.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'If + had + V3, would have + V3' – past unreal.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence demonstrates correct use of 'could have + past participle' for a past possibility that didn't happen?
options: You could have told me earlier.
You could be telling me earlier.
You could have been telling me earlier.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Could have + V3' – past possibility (regret/criticism).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'might have + past participle' to express uncertainty about the past?
options: He might have been leaving already.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Might have + V3' – past uncertainty.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence where 'must have + past participle' expresses a logical conclusion about the past.
options: He must have finished by now.
He must be finishing by now.
He must have been finishing by now.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Must have + V3' – logical deduction about past.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'can't have + past participle' correctly for negative deduction?
options: He can't have seen us – it was too dark.
He can't see us – it was too dark.
He couldn't have seen us – it was too dark.
He couldn't see us – it was too dark.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Can't have + V3' – negative deduction about past.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence demonstrates the correct use of 'needn't have + past participle'?
options: You needn't have bought any bread – we had plenty.
You needn't buy any bread – we had plenty.
You didn't need to buy bread – we had plenty.
You needn't have bought any bread – we have plenty.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Needn't have + V3' – unnecessary past action (already done).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'didn't need to' to mean the action was not necessary and was not done?
options: I didn't need to cook because we ate out.
I needn't have cooked because we ate out.
I didn't need to cook but I did.
I needn't cook because we ate out.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Didn't need to' – action not necessary and not done.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence where 'had better' expresses a strong warning rather than advice.
options: You had better finish your homework.
You had better not be late again.
You had better see a doctor.
You had better take an umbrella.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Had better not' – strong warning (threat).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'would rather' with a past reference?
options: I would rather you had not told him.
I would rather you didn't tell him.
I would rather you not tell him.
I would rather you haven't told him.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Would rather + subject + had + V3' – past regret.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly distinguishes 'like' and 'as' in a comparison?
options: He speaks like a native speaker does.
He speaks as a native speaker.
He speaks like a native speaker.
He speaks as like a native speaker.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Like' + noun (comparison), 'as' + clause.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'as if' for an unreal situation in the present?
options: He talks as if he knows everything.
He talks as if he knew everything.
He talks as if he has known everything.
He talks as if he is knowing everything.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'As if + past subjunctive' – unreal present.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence where 'even if' introduces a hypothetical concession.
options: Even if it rains, we will go.
Even if it rained, we would go.
Even if it had rained, we would have gone.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Even if' – hypothetical concession in all time frames.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'only if' for a strong condition?
options: Only if you apologize will I forgive you.
Only if you apologize I will forgive you.
Only if you apologize, I will forgive you.
Only if you apologize will I forgive you?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Only if + clause + auxiliary + subject' – inversion.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'provided that' with a future reference?
options: Provided that you will come, I'll stay.
Provided that you come, I'll stay.
Provided that you came, I'll stay.
Provided that you have come, I'll stay.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Provided that + present simple' for future condition.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence where 'suppose' introduces a hypothetical situation.
options: Suppose you won the lottery, what would you do?
Suppose you win the lottery, what will you do?
Suppose you have won the lottery, what will you do?
Suppose you are winning the lottery, what will you do?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Suppose + past tense' for hypothetical.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'what if' for a worrying possibility?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'What if + present simple' – real worry.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'as long as' for a condition?
options: You can stay as long as you are quiet.
You can stay as long as you will be quiet.
You can stay as long as you were quiet.
You can stay as long as you have been quiet.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'As long as + present simple' – condition.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence demonstrates the correct use of 'on condition that' with a negative?
options: I'll lend you the money on condition that you not waste it.
I'll lend you the money on condition that you don't waste it.
I'll lend you the money on condition that you won't waste it.
I'll lend you the money on condition that you didn't waste it.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'On condition that + don't' for negative condition.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'but for' in a past conditional?
options: But for your help, I would have failed.
But for your help, I would fail.
But for your help, I failed.
But for your help, I have failed.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'But for + noun, would have + V3' – past conditional.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'if it were not for' in a present context?
options: If it were not for your help, I would be lost.
If it were not for your help, I would have been lost.
If it had not been for your help, I would be lost.
If it was not for your help, I would be lost.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'If it were not for' + would – present unreal.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'if only' to express a past wish?
options: If only I had studied harder.
If only I will study harder.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'If only + had + V3' – past regret.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'it's time' with a past subjunctive?
options: It's time we will go home.
It's time we have gone home.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'It's time + past subjunctive' – suggestion for now.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'it's high time' for criticism?
options: It's high time you started working harder.
It's high time you start working harder.
It's high time you will start working harder.
It's high time you have started working harder.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'It's high time + past subjunctive' – criticism.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'for fear that' with 'might'?
options: He left early for fear that he might miss the train.
He left early for fear that he will miss the train.
He left early for fear that he misses the train.
He left early for fear that he missed the train.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'For fear that + might' – past concern.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'lest' with the subjunctive?
options: He ran lest he be late.
He ran lest he is late.
He ran lest he was late.
He ran lest he will be late.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Lest + subjunctive (be)' – formal negative purpose.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'in case of' with a noun phrase?
options: In case of fire, leave the building.
In case of fire, you will leave the building.
In case of fire, leaving the building.
In case of fire, to leave the building.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In case of + noun' – emergency instructions.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'in the event of' formally?
options: In the event of an emergency, call 911.
In the event of an emergency, you will call 911.
In the event of an emergency, calling 911.
In the event of an emergency, called 911.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In the event of + noun' – formal contingency.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'with a view to' with a gerund?
options: He bought the land with a view to building a house.
He bought the land with a view to build a house.
He bought the land with a view to built a house.
He bought the land with a view to will build a house.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'With a view to + V-ing' – purpose.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'so as to' with a negative infinitive?
options: He left early so as not to miss the train.
He left early so as to not miss the train.
He left early so as to don't miss the train.
He left early so as to won't miss the train.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'So as not to + infinitive' – negative purpose.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'in order that' with 'might'?
options: He spoke slowly in order that everyone might understand.
He spoke slowly in order that everyone will understand.
He spoke slowly in order that everyone understands.
He spoke slowly in order that everyone understood.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In order that + might' – formal purpose.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'seeing that' for an obvious reason?
options: Seeing that it's raining, we should stay home.
Seeing that it rains, we should stay home.
Seeing that it rained, we should stay home.
Seeing that it will rain, we should stay home.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Seeing that + present' – obvious reason.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'given that' for a known fact?
options: Given that he is an expert, his opinion matters.
Given that he was an expert, his opinion mattered.
Given that he will be an expert, his opinion will matter.
Given that he has been an expert, his opinion has mattered.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Given that + present' – known fact.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'in that' for a specific reason in academic writing?
options: The theory is flawed in that it ignores historical context.
The theory is flawed in that, it ignores historical context.
The theory is flawed in that which ignores historical context.
The theory is flawed in that where it ignores historical context.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In that + clause' – specific reason (academic).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'now that' for a new situation?
options: Now that you are here, we can start.
Now that you were here, we could start.
Now that you will be here, we will start.
Now that you have been here, we started.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Now that + present' – new situation.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'insofar as' to limit a claim?
options: He is correct insofar as I can judge.
He is correct insofar as I can judge?
He is correct insofar as I can judge!
He is correct insofar as I can judge;
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Insofar as' – to the extent that (formal).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'for all' to mean 'despite'?
options: For all his wealth, he is unhappy.
For all his wealth he is unhappy.
For all his wealth, but he is unhappy.
For all his wealth, so he is unhappy.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'For all + noun' = despite.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'much as' for concession?
options: Much as I admire him, I cannot agree.
Much as I admire him I cannot agree.
Much as I admire him, but I cannot agree.
Much as I admire him, so I cannot agree.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Much as + clause' = although.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'be that as it may' for concession?
options: Be that as it may, we must continue.
Be that as it may we must continue.
Be that as it may, but we must continue.
Be that as it may, so we must continue.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Be that as it may' – formal concession.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'nevertheless' with a semicolon?
options: It was raining; nevertheless, we went out.
It was raining nevertheless, we went out.
It was raining, nevertheless we went out.
It was raining nevertheless we went out.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: '; nevertheless,' – formal contrast.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'nonetheless' for contrast?
options: It was difficult; nonetheless, we succeeded.
It was difficult nonetheless, we succeeded.
It was difficult, nonetheless we succeeded.
It was difficult nonetheless we succeeded.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: '; nonetheless,' – formal contrast.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'notwithstanding' in postpositive position?
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Noun + notwithstanding' – most formal.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'regardless of' with a gerund?
options: Regardless of being tired, he finished.
Regardless of he was tired, he finished.
Regardless of his tiredness, he finished.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Regardless of + gerund' – despite.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'irrespective of' with a noun?
options: Irrespective of age, everyone can apply.
Irrespective of age everyone can apply.
Irrespective of age, everyone can apply
Irrespective of age everyone can apply.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Irrespective of + noun' – regardless of.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'in light of' with a past event?
options: In light of recent events, we changed our plans.
In light of recent events we changed our plans.
In light of recent events, we change our plans.
In light of recent events we change our plans.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In light of + noun' – because of (past result).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'owing to' in formal writing?
options: Owing to the rain, the match was cancelled.
Owing to the rain the match was cancelled.
Owing to the rain, the match is cancelled.
Owing to the rain the match is cancelled.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Owing to + noun' – because of (formal).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'thanks to' for a positive reason?
options: Thanks to your help, we succeeded.
Thanks to your help we succeeded.
Thanks to your help, we succeed.
Thanks to your help we succeed.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Thanks to + noun' – positive cause.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'by virtue of' in a legal context?
options: He holds power by virtue of his position.
He holds power by virtue of his position?
He holds power by virtue of his position!
He holds power by virtue of his position;
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'By virtue of + noun' – because of (legal/formal).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'in view of' with a future plan?
options: In view of the weather, we will stay home.
In view of the weather we will stay home.
In view of the weather, we stay home.
In view of the weather we stay home.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In view of + noun' – considering.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'on account of' with a gerund?
options: He was late on account of missing the bus.
He was late on account of he missed the bus.
He was late on account of his missing the bus.
He was late on account of missed the bus.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'On account of + gerund' – because of.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'in consequence of' formally?
options: In consequence of his actions, he was fired.
In consequence of his actions he was fired.
In consequence of his actions, he is fired.
In consequence of his actions he is fired.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'In consequence of + noun' – as a result of.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'as a result of' with a noun phrase?
options: As a result of the storm, many trees fell.
As a result of the storm many trees fell.
As a result of the storm, many trees fall.
As a result of the storm many trees fall.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'As a result of + noun' – cause-effect.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'due to' after a form of 'to be'?
options: The cancellation was due to the rain.
Due to the rain, the game was cancelled.
The game was cancelled due to the rain.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Due to' – all positions acceptable now.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'the same...as' with an inverted clause?
options: He has the same car as does his brother.
He has the same car as his brother does.
He has the same car as his brother.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'The same...as' – inversion possible.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'different from' with a pronoun?
options: His opinion is different from mine.
His opinion is different than mine.
His opinion is different to mine.
His opinion is different of mine.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Different from' – standard English.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'similar to' with a clause?
options: His approach is similar to that of his predecessor.
His approach is similar to his predecessor.
His approach is similar like his predecessor.
His approach is similar as his predecessor.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Similar to that of' – precise comparison.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'such as' with a list?
options: He likes sports such as football, tennis, and basketball.
He likes sports such as football, tennis and basketball.
He likes sports such as: football, tennis, basketball.
He likes sports such as football tennis basketball.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Such as' – comma before list, 'and' before last.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'rather than' with parallel structure?
options: He chose to quit rather than to continue.
He chose to quit rather than continue.
He chose quitting rather than continuing.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Rather than' – parallel structures.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'no less than' for emphasis?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'No less than' – emphatic quantity.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'nothing less than' for strong emotion?
options: His behavior was nothing less than outrageous.
His behavior was nothing less than outrageous?
His behavior was nothing less than outrageous!
His behavior was nothing less than outrageous;
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Nothing less than' – completely (strong).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'all but' to mean 'almost'?
options: He was all but dead when they found him.
He was all but dead when they found him?
He was all but dead when they found him!
He was all but dead when they found him;
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'All but + adjective' = almost.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'anything but' for negation?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Anything but + adjective' = not at all.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'nothing but' for restriction?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Nothing but + noun' = only.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'more than' with an adjective for emphasis?
options: He is more than happy to help.
He is more than happy to help?
He is more than happy to help!
He is more than happy to help;
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'More than + adjective' = very.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'sooner or later' for inevitability?
options: Sooner or later, everyone makes mistakes.
Sooner or later everyone makes mistakes.
Sooner or later, everyone makes mistakes
Sooner or later everyone makes mistakes.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Sooner or later' – inevitable future.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'as it were' as a hedging device?
options: He is, as it were, a walking encyclopedia.
He is as it were a walking encyclopedia.
He is, as it were a walking encyclopedia.
He is as it were, a walking encyclopedia.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'As it were' – hedging (formal).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'so to speak' for figurative language?
options: He is, so to speak, a lone wolf.
He is so to speak a lone wolf.
He is, so to speak a lone wolf.
He is so to speak, a lone wolf.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'So to speak' – figurative marker.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'in a manner of speaking'?
options: He is, in a manner of speaking, the leader.
He is in a manner of speaking the leader.
He is, in a manner of speaking the leader.
He is in a manner of speaking, the leader.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'In a manner of speaking' – loosely speaking.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'to say the least' for understatement?
options: His behavior was inappropriate, to say the least.
His behavior was inappropriate to say the least.
His behavior was inappropriate, to say the least
His behavior was inappropriate to say the least.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'To say the least' – understatement.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'needless to say'?
options: Needless to say, he was late again.
Needless to say he was late again.
Needless to say, he was late again
Needless to say he was late again.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Needless to say' – obvious statement.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'strange as it may seem'?
options: Strange as it may seem, he passed the test.
Strange as it may seem he passed the test.
Strange as it may seem, he passed the test
Strange as it may seem he passed the test.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Strange as it may seem' – concession.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'believe it or not'?
options: Believe it or not, I have never seen snow.
Believe it or not I have never seen snow.
Believe it or not, I have never seen snow
Believe it or not I have never seen snow.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Believe it or not' – surprising truth.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'if you ask me' for opinion?
options: If you ask me, he is making a mistake.
If you ask me he is making a mistake.
If you ask me, he is making a mistake
If you ask me he is making a mistake.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'If you ask me' – personal opinion.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'mind you' to add a contrasting point?
options: He is very smart. Mind you, he can be lazy.
He is very smart. Mind you he can be lazy.
He is very smart mind you, he can be lazy.
He is very smart mind you he can be lazy.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Mind you' – contrast marker.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'still' as a discourse marker for contrast?
options: He is rich. Still, he is unhappy.
He is rich still, he is unhappy.
He is rich still he is unhappy.
He is rich, still he is unhappy.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Still' – contrast marker (nonetheless).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'though' at the end of a sentence for contrast?
options: He is rich. He is unhappy, though.
He is rich. He is unhappy though.
He is rich. Though, he is unhappy.
He is rich though, he is unhappy.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Though' at end – informal contrast.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'whereas' to contrast two clauses?
options: He likes coffee, whereas she prefers tea.
He likes coffee whereas she prefers tea.
He likes coffee whereas, she prefers tea.
He likes coffee, whereas she prefers tea.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Whereas' – direct contrast.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'while' for contrast (not time)?
options: While he is rich, she is poor.
While he is rich she is poor.
While he is rich, she is poor
While he is rich she is poor.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'While' for contrast – comma.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'on the contrary' after a negative?
options: He is not lazy. On the contrary, he works very hard.
He is not lazy. On the contrary he works very hard.
He is not lazy on the contrary, he works very hard.
He is not lazy on the contrary he works very hard.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'On the contrary' – after negative.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'in contrast' with 'to'?
options: In contrast to his brother, he is tall.
In contrast with his brother, he is tall.
In contrast of his brother, he is tall.
In contrast for his brother, he is tall.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In contrast to + noun' – standard.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'by contrast' without 'to'?
options: He is tall. By contrast, his brother is short.
He is tall. By contrast his brother is short.
He is tall by contrast, his brother is short.
He is tall by contrast his brother is short.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'By contrast' – no 'to'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'conversely' for opposite relationship?
options: Increasing price reduces demand. Conversely, lowering price increases demand.
Increasing price reduces demand. Conversely lowering price increases demand.
Increasing price reduces demand conversely, lowering price increases demand.
Increasing price reduces demand conversely lowering price increases demand.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Conversely' – opposite logical relationship.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence correctly uses 'as opposed to' for clear distinction?
options: He prefers coffee as opposed to tea.
He prefers coffee as opposed to tea?
He prefers coffee as opposed to tea!
He prefers coffee as opposed to tea;
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'As opposed to' – explicit contrast.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct passive form: 'They are building a new hospital.'
options: A new hospital is being built.
A new hospital was being built.
A new hospital has been built.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Present continuous passive: is being + V3.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'had better' correctly?
options: You had better to see a doctor.
You had better seeing a doctor.
You had better see a doctor.
You had better saw a doctor.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Had better + infinitive without to'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'prefer' and 'would rather'.
options: I would rather coffee than tea.
I would rather to drink coffee.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Prefer something to something'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'as if' correctly for unreal present?
options: He talks as if he knows everything.
He talks as if he knew everything.
He talks as if he has known everything.
He talks as if he is knowing everything.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'As if + past subjunctive' for unreal present.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct inversion with 'not only...but also'.
options: Not only he is intelligent, but also hardworking.
Not only is he intelligent, but also hardworking.
Not only he intelligent is, but also hardworking.
Not only he is intelligent, but also he is hardworking.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Not only + auxiliary + subject + verb' – inversion.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'by the time' correctly with past perfect?
options: By the time we arrived, they left.
By the time we arrived, they have left.
By the time we arrived, they had left.
By the time we arrived, they were leaving.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'By the time + past simple, past perfect'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'so that' for purpose.
options: He saved money so that he can buy a car.
He saved money so that he could buy a car.
He saved money so that he buys a car.
He saved money so that he will buy a car.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'So that + could/would' for past purpose.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'given that' correctly?
options: Given that he is tired, he finished the work.
Given that he was tired, he finished the work.
Given that he tired, he finished the work.
Given that he has tired, he finished the work.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Given that + past tense' for known past fact.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'what' as a fused relative pronoun.
options: That what he said was interesting.
The thing what he said was interesting.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'What' = 'the thing(s) that' – fused relative.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'however' correctly as a conjunction?
options: He is rich, however he is unhappy.
He is rich however, he is unhappy.
He is rich; however, he is unhappy.
He is rich however he is unhappy.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: '; however,' connects two clauses.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'no matter'.
options: No matter how hard he tries, he never succeeds.
No matter how hard he try, he never succeeds.
No matter how hard he trying, he never succeeds.
No matter hard he tries, he never succeeds.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'No matter how + adjective/adverb + subject + verb'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'seeing that' correctly?
options: Seeing that it's raining, we should stay home.
Seeing that it rains, we should stay home.
Seeing that it rained, we should stay home.
Seeing that it will rain, we should stay home.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Seeing that + present' = because (obvious reason).

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'such that'.
options: His anger was such that everyone was scared.
His anger was such so that everyone was scared.
His anger was such as that everyone was scared.
His anger was such to that everyone was scared.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Such that' = to such a degree that.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'in that' correctly?
options: This book is useful in that it explains grammar clearly.
This book is useful in that it will explain grammar clearly.
This book is useful in that it explained grammar clearly.
This book is useful in that it has explained grammar clearly.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In that + present' = because (formal, specific reason).

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'were it not for'.
options: Were it not for your help, I would fail.
Were it not for your help, I will fail.
Were it not for your help, I failed.
Were it not for your help, I have failed.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Were it not for + noun, would + infinitive' – present unreal.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'had it not been for' correctly?
options: Had it not been for the rain, we would have arrived on time.
Had it not been for the rain, we will arrive on time.
Had it not been for the rain, we arrived on time.
Had it not been for the rain, we have arrived on time.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Had it not been for + noun, would have + V3' – past unreal.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'on the contrary'.
options: He is not lazy. On the contrary, he works very hard.
He is not lazy. On the contrary he works very hard.
He is not lazy on the contrary, he works very hard.
He is not lazy on the contrary he works very hard.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'On the contrary' after a negative statement.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'in contrast' with 'to' correctly?
options: In contrast to his brother, he is tall.
In contrast with his brother, he is tall.
In contrast of his brother, he is tall.
In contrast for his brother, he is tall.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In contrast to + noun'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'whereas'.
options: He likes coffee, whereas she prefers tea.
He likes coffee whereas she prefers tea.
He likes coffee whereas, she prefers tea.
He likes coffee, whereas she prefers tea.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Whereas' connects two contrasting clauses.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'albeit' correctly?
options: He accepted the job, albeit with some hesitation.
He accepted the job albeit with some hesitation.
He accepted the job, albeit he hesitated.
He accepted the job albeit he hesitated.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Albeit + phrase' – formal concession.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'for all'.
options: For all his wealth, he is unhappy.
For all his wealth he is unhappy.
For all his wealth, but he is unhappy.
For all his wealth, so he is unhappy.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'For all + noun' = despite.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'much as' correctly?
options: Much as I admire him, I cannot agree with him.
Much as I admire him I cannot agree with him.
Much as I admire him, but I cannot agree.
Much as I admire him, so I cannot agree.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Much as + clause' = although.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'be that as it may'.
options: Be that as it may, we must continue.
Be that as it may we must continue.
Be that as it may, but we must continue.
Be that as it may, so we must continue.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Be that as it may' – formal concession.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'insofar as' correctly?
options: He is correct insofar as I can judge.
He is correct insofar as I can judge?
He is correct insofar as I can judge!
He is correct insofar as I can judge;
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Insofar as' = to the extent that.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'with a view to'.
options: He is saving money with a view to buying a house.
He is saving money with a view to buy a house.
He is saving money with a view to bought a house.
He is saving money with a view to will buy a house.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'With a view to + V-ing'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'for fear that' correctly?
options: He left early for fear that he might miss the train.
He left early for fear that he will miss the train.
He left early for fear that he misses the train.
He left early for fear that he missed the train.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'For fear that + might' – past concern.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'lest'.
options: He ran lest he be late.
He ran lest he is late.
He ran lest he was late.
He ran lest he will be late.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Lest + subjunctive (be)' – formal negative purpose.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'in the event that' correctly?
options: In the event that it rains, the ceremony will be indoors.
In the event that it will rain, the ceremony will be indoors.
In the event that it rained, the ceremony will be indoors.
In the event that it has rained, the ceremony will be indoors.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In the event that + present simple'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'provided that'.
options: You can go out provided that you finish your homework.
You can go out provided that you will finish your homework.
You can go out provided that you finished your homework.
You can go out provided that you have finished your homework.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Provided that + present simple'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'as long as' correctly?
options: You can stay as long as you are quiet.
You can stay as long as you will be quiet.
You can stay as long as you were quiet.
You can stay as long as you have been quiet.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'As long as + present simple' for condition.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'on the grounds that'.
options: He was fired on the grounds that he was dishonest.
He was fired on the grounds that he is dishonest.
He was fired on the grounds that he will be dishonest.
He was fired on the grounds that he has been dishonest.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'On the grounds that + past tense'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'in light of' correctly?
options: In light of recent events, we have changed our plans.
In light of recent events we have changed our plans.
In light of recent events, we changed our plans.
In light of recent events we changed our plans.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In light of + noun' = because of.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'owing to'.
options: Owing to the rain, the match was cancelled.
Owing to the rain the match was cancelled.
Owing to the rain, the match is cancelled.
Owing to the rain the match is cancelled.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Owing to + noun' = because of (formal).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'thanks to' correctly?
options: Thanks to your help, we succeeded.
Thanks to your help we succeeded.
Thanks to your help, we succeed.
Thanks to your help we succeed.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Thanks to + noun' – positive cause.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'by virtue of'.
options: He holds power by virtue of his position.
He holds power by virtue of his position?
He holds power by virtue of his position!
He holds power by virtue of his position;
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'By virtue of + noun' = because of (legal/formal).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'in view of' correctly?
options: In view of the weather, we stayed home.
In view of the weather we stayed home.
In view of the weather, we stay home.
In view of the weather we stay home.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In view of + noun' = considering.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'on account of'.
options: He was late on account of traffic.
He was late on account of traffic?
He was late on account of traffic!
He was late on account of traffic;
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'On account of + noun' = because of.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'in consequence of' correctly?
options: In consequence of his actions, he was fired.
In consequence of his actions he was fired.
In consequence of his actions, he is fired.
In consequence of his actions he is fired.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'In consequence of + noun' = as a result of.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'as a result of'.
options: As a result of the storm, many trees fell.
As a result of the storm many trees fell.
As a result of the storm, many trees fall.
As a result of the storm many trees fall.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'As a result of + noun' – cause-effect.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'due to' correctly?
options: The cancellation was due to the rain.
Due to the rain, the game was cancelled.
The game was cancelled due to the rain.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Due to' – now acceptable in all positions.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'in order that'.
options: He left early in order that he might catch the train.
He left early in order that he will catch the train.
He left early in order that he catches the train.
He left early in order that he caught the train.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In order that + might/could' – formal purpose.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'so as to' correctly?
options: He left early so as to catch the train.
He left early so as to catching the train.
He left early so as to caught the train.
He left early so as to will catch the train.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'So as to + infinitive' – purpose.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'with the intention of'.
options: He went to London with the intention of finding a job.
He went to London with the intention to find a job.
He went to London with the intention for finding a job.
He went to London with the intention that find a job.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'With the intention of + V-ing'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'for the purpose of' correctly?
options: This tool is used for the purpose of cutting metal.
This tool is used for the purpose to cut metal.
This tool is used for the purpose that cut metal.
This tool is used for the purpose of cut metal.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'For the purpose of + V-ing'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'in the hope of' correctly?
options: He studied hard in the hope of passing the exam.
He studied hard in the hope to pass the exam.
He studied hard in the hope for passing the exam.
He studied hard in the hope that pass the exam.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In the hope of + V-ing'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'for fear of'.
options: He didn't speak for fear of making a mistake.
He didn't speak for fear to make a mistake.
He didn't speak for fear for making a mistake.
He didn't speak for fear that make a mistake.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'For fear of + V-ing'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'in case of' correctly?
options: In case of fire, leave the building.
In case of fire, you will leave the building.
In case of fire, you leave the building.
In case of fire, leaving the building.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In case of + noun' – emergency instructions.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'in the event of'.
options: In the event of an emergency, call 911.
In the event of an emergency, you will call 911.
In the event of an emergency, calling 911.
In the event of an emergency, called 911.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In the event of + noun' – formal contingency.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'but for' correctly?
options: But for your help, I would have failed.
But for your help, I will fail.
But for your help, I failed.
But for your help, I have failed.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'But for + noun, would have + V3' – past conditional.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'if only'.
options: If only I had studied harder.
If only I will study harder.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'If only + had + V3' – past regret.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'it's time' correctly?
options: It's time we will go home.
It's time we have gone home.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'It's time + past subjunctive' – suggestion.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'would rather'.
options: I would rather you didn't smoke.
I would rather you don't smoke.
I would rather you won't smoke.
I would rather you haven't smoked.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Would rather + subject + past tense' – preference about others.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'had better' correctly?
options: You had better not be late.
You had better not to be late.
You had better not being late.
You had better not been late.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Had better + infinitive without to'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'as it were'.
options: He is, as it were, a walking dictionary.
He is as it were a walking dictionary.
He is, as it were a walking dictionary.
He is as it were, a walking dictionary.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'As it were' – hedging (formal).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'so to speak' correctly?
options: He is, so to speak, a lone wolf.
He is so to speak a lone wolf.
He is, so to speak a lone wolf.
He is so to speak, a lone wolf.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'So to speak' – figurative marker.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'in a manner of speaking'.
options: He is, in a manner of speaking, the leader.
He is in a manner of speaking the leader.
He is, in a manner of speaking the leader.
He is in a manner of speaking, the leader.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'In a manner of speaking' – loosely speaking.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'to say the least' correctly?
options: His behavior was inappropriate, to say the least.
His behavior was inappropriate to say the least.
His behavior was inappropriate, to say the least
His behavior was inappropriate to say the least.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'To say the least' – understatement.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'needless to say'.
options: Needless to say, he was late again.
Needless to say he was late again.
Needless to say, he was late again
Needless to say he was late again.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Needless to say' – obvious statement.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'strange as it may seem' correctly?
options: Strange as it may seem, he passed the test.
Strange as it may seem he passed the test.
Strange as it may seem, he passed the test
Strange as it may seem he passed the test.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Strange as it may seem' – concession.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'believe it or not'.
options: Believe it or not, I have never seen snow.
Believe it or not I have never seen snow.
Believe it or not, I have never seen snow
Believe it or not I have never seen snow.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Believe it or not' – surprising truth.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'if you ask me' correctly?
options: If you ask me, he is making a mistake.
If you ask me he is making a mistake.
If you ask me, he is making a mistake
If you ask me he is making a mistake.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'If you ask me' – personal opinion.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'mind you'.
options: He is very smart. Mind you, he can be lazy.
He is very smart. Mind you he can be lazy.
He is very smart mind you, he can be lazy.
He is very smart mind you he can be lazy.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Mind you' – contrast marker.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'still' as a discourse marker correctly?
options: He is rich. Still, he is unhappy.
He is rich still, he is unhappy.
He is rich still he is unhappy.
He is rich, still he is unhappy.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Still' – contrast marker (nonetheless).

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'nevertheless'.
options: It was raining; nevertheless, we went out.
It was raining nevertheless, we went out.
It was raining, nevertheless we went out.
It was raining nevertheless we went out.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: '; nevertheless,' – formal contrast.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'nonetheless' correctly?
options: It was difficult; nonetheless, we succeeded.
It was difficult nonetheless, we succeeded.
It was difficult, nonetheless we succeeded.
It was difficult nonetheless we succeeded.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: '; nonetheless,' – formal contrast.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'notwithstanding'.
options: Notwithstanding the rain, we went out.
Notwithstanding the rain we went out.
Notwithstanding the rain, we went out
Notwithstanding the rain we went out.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Notwithstanding + noun' = despite (formal).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'regardless of' correctly?
options: Regardless of the cost, we must do it.
Regardless of the cost we must do it.
Regardless of the cost, we must do it
Regardless of the cost we must do it.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Regardless of + noun'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'irrespective of'.
options: Irrespective of age, everyone can apply.
Irrespective of age everyone can apply.
Irrespective of age, everyone can apply
Irrespective of age everyone can apply.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Irrespective of + noun' = regardless of.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'in spite of' correctly?
options: In spite of the rain, we enjoyed the day.
In spite of the rain we enjoyed the day.
In spite of the rain, we enjoyed the day
In spite of the rain we enjoyed the day.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'In spite of + noun/gerund'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the sentence with correct use of 'despite'.
options: Despite being tired, he finished the work.
Despite being tired he finished the work.
Despite being tired, he finished the work
Despite being tired he finished the work.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Despite + noun/gerund'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence uses 'whereas' correctly?
options: He likes coffee, whereas she likes tea.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The plan ____ into action.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Put into action', 'set into action', 'put into motion'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a promise'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make a promise', 'keep a promise', 'break a promise'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a new theory.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Propose a theory', 'suggest a theory', 'put forward a theory'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which adjective collocates with 'difference'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: All mean 'large/important difference'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ a difficult time.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Go through a difficult time', 'experience a difficult time', 'endure a difficult time'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a conversation'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Have a conversation', 'hold a conversation', 'conduct a conversation'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The evidence is ____.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: All mean 'believable' with 'evidence'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which noun collocates with 'heavy' meaning 'serious'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Heavy responsibility', 'heavy burdens', 'heavy losses'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a strong opinion.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Hold an opinion', 'have an opinion', 'maintain an opinion'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'an attempt'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make an attempt', 'mount an attempt', 'launch an attempt'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The project was a ____ success.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Resounding success', 'great success', 'remarkable success'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which adjective collocates with 'need'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Pressing need', 'urgent need', 'critical need'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ her anger.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Control anger', 'suppress anger', 'manage anger'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a problem'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Address a problem', 'tackle a problem', 'solve a problem'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a new record.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Set a record', 'establish a record', 'create a record'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which noun collocates with 'deep' meaning 'strong'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Deep affection', 'deep respect', 'deep concern'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The government ____ new taxes.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Impose taxes', 'levy taxes', 'introduce taxes'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a decision'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make a decision', 'take a decision' (British), 'reach a decision'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ a high price.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Pay a price', 'charge a price', 'demand a price'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which adjective collocates with 'mistake'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Serious mistake', 'grave mistake', 'costly mistake'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The situation is ____.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Critical situation', 'urgent situation', 'serious situation'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'an opportunity'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Seize an opportunity', 'take an opportunity', 'grasp an opportunity'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a significant contribution.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make a contribution' or 'give a contribution'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which noun collocates with 'bitter' meaning 'intense'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Bitter struggle', 'bitter conflict', 'bitter feud'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The report ____ the main issues.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Highlight issues', 'emphasize issues', 'stress issues'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a proposal'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make a proposal', 'submit a proposal', 'reject a proposal'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ a new skill.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Acquire a skill', 'develop a skill', 'master a skill'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which adjective collocates with 'damage'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Severe damage', 'extensive damage', 'irreparable damage'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a valid excuse.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Give an excuse', 'provide an excuse', 'offer an excuse'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a law'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Pass a law', 'enact a law', 'violate a law'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The company ____ a new CEO.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Appoint a CEO', 'name a CEO', 'hire a CEO'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which noun collocates with 'deep' meaning 'extreme'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Deep depression', 'deep sorrow', 'deep crisis'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ a good salary.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Earn a salary', 'make a salary', 'receive a salary'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a challenge'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Face a challenge', 'meet a challenge', 'overcome a challenge'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The policy ____ controversy.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Cause controversy', 'generate controversy', 'spark controversy'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which adjective collocates with 'evidence'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Clear evidence', 'strong evidence', 'overwhelming evidence'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a good impression.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make an impression' or 'create an impression'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a plan'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Devise a plan', 'formulate a plan', 'implement a plan'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ a lot of support.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Receive support', 'gain support', 'garner support'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which noun collocates with 'heavy' meaning 'large amount'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Heavy rainfall', 'heavy snowfall', 'heavy casualties'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The project ____ funding.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Receive funding', 'secure funding', 'obtain funding'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a meeting'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Hold a meeting', 'attend a meeting', 'schedule a meeting'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a difficult decision.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make a decision', 'face a decision', 'take a decision'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which adjective collocates with 'increase'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Significant increase', 'substantial increase', 'dramatic increase'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ a business.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Start a business', 'set up a business', 'establish a business'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a question'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Ask a question', 'raise a question', 'pose a question'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The two companies ____ a merger.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Announce a merger', 'declare a merger', 'propose a merger'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which noun collocates with 'deep' meaning 'profound'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Deep understanding', 'deep knowledge', 'deep insight'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a lot of money.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make money', 'earn money', 'invest money'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a test'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Take a test', 'pass a test', 'fail a test'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ her career.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Build a career', 'develop a career', 'advance a career'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which adjective collocates with 'risk'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'High risk', 'considerable risk', 'significant risk'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The news ____ a shock.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Come as a shock' or 'cause a shock'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a fire'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Start a fire', 'extinguish a fire', 'put out a fire'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a good example.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Set an example', 'show an example', 'provide an example'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which noun collocates with 'heavy' meaning 'intense'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Heavy fighting', 'heavy criticism', 'heavy competition'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ a degree.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Earn a degree', 'obtain a degree', 'receive a degree'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a holiday'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Take a holiday', 'spend a holiday', 'book a holiday'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The project ____ a delay.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Experience a delay', 'suffer a delay', 'face a delay'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which adjective collocates with 'need' meaning 'urgent'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Desperate need', 'crying need', 'pressing need'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a mistake.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make a mistake' or 'commit a mistake'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a request'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make a request', 'submit a request', 'grant a request'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ a new product.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Launch a product', 'develop a product', 'introduce a product'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which noun collocates with 'deep' meaning 'serious'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Deep trouble', 'deep problem', 'deep crisis'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'The team ____ a goal.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Score a goal' (sports), 'achieve a goal', 'reach a goal'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb collocates with 'a relationship'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Build a relationship', 'maintain a relationship', 'end a relationship'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'He ____ a fortune in real estate.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Make a fortune', 'amass a fortune', 'accumulate a fortune'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which adjective collocates with 'change'?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Dramatic change', 'radical change', 'fundamental change'.

ingliz_tili.text: Choose the correct collocation: 'She ____ a lot of experience.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Gain experience', 'acquire experience', 'develop experience'.

ingliz_tili.text: A: 'Did you finish the report?' B: 'I've been sick all week.' Which Gricean maxim does B violate?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Relevance maxim – B's answer doesn't directly address whether the report was finished.

ingliz_tili.text: Which utterance is an example of a speech act of promising?
options: I promise to pay you back.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'I promise' explicitly performs the act of promising (performative verb).

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence contains a presupposition?
options: The king of France is bald.
Is the king of France bald?
Don't be bald like the king of France.
If the king of France is bald, he should wear a hat.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Presupposes 'There is a king of France' (existential presupposition).

ingliz_tili.text: What is deixis?
options: Words that refer to shared knowledge
Words that point to time, place, or person in context
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Deictic words: here, there, now, then, I, you, this, that.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of negative politeness strategy?
options: I'm sorry to bother you, but could you possibly close the door?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Negative politeness respects hearer's need to be unimpeded (apologetic, indirect).

ingliz_tili.text: Which utterance shows an indirect speech act?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Can you pass the salt?' is a question about ability, but functions as a request.

ingliz_tili.text: What is implicature?
options: What is implied but not said
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Implicature is additional meaning inferred from context, not literally expressed.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of a face-threatening act (FTA)?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'You are wrong' threatens the hearer's positive face (desire to be approved of).

ingliz_tili.text: What does entailment mean in pragmatics?
options: One sentence logically follows from another
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: If A entails B, then if A is true, B must also be true.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence has a performative verb?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Promise' is a performative verb – saying it performs the action.

ingliz_tili.text: What is the cooperative principle?
options: Speakers should always tell the truth
Speakers should contribute appropriately to the conversation
Speakers should use only literal meanings
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Grice's cooperative principle: make your contribution as required by the conversation.

ingliz_tili.text: Which violates the maxim of quality?
options: I have two brothers (when you have three).
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Maxim of quality: be truthful. A and C are false statements.

ingliz_tili.text: Which violates the maxim of quantity?
options: A: 'What time is it?' B: 'It's daytime.'
A: 'Where do you live?' B: 'In London.'
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Maxim of quantity: be as informative as needed. B gave less information than required.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a felicity condition?
options: Conditions for a sentence to be grammatical
Conditions for a speech act to be appropriate and successful
Conditions for a word to be pronounced correctly
Conditions for a text to be coherent
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Felicity conditions: e.g., to promise, the speaker must intend to do the action.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of positive politeness?
options: Could you possibly open the window?
You're so kind. Would you mind opening the window?
I'm afraid I have to ask you to open the window.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Positive politeness attends to hearer's positive face (compliment, solidarity).

ingliz_tili.text: What is anaphora?
options: Referring back to something previously mentioned
Referring forward to something not yet mentioned
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Anaphora: 'John came late. He was tired.' – 'he' refers back to 'John'.

ingliz_tili.text: What is cataphora?
options: Referring back to something previously mentioned
Referring forward to something not yet mentioned
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Cataphora: 'After he arrived, John sat down.' – 'he' refers forward to 'John'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which sentence contains a bridging inference (indirect anaphora)?
options: John bought a car. The engine was powerful.
John bought a car. It was red.
John and Mary arrived. They were tired.
John saw a dog. The dog was barking.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'The engine' – no direct mention, but hearer infers cars have engines.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a scalar implicature?
options: Some of the students passed the exam. (implies not all)
The sun rises in the east.
If it rains, the ground gets wet.
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Some' implicates 'not all' on the scale <all, most, many, some>.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of a conventional implicature?
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'But' conventionally implicates contrast between being rich and being honest.

ingliz_tili.text: What is the difference between implicature and entailment?
options: Implicature is cancellable, entailment is not
Entailment is cancellable, implicature is not
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: Implicature can be cancelled ('Some passed, in fact all did'), entailment cannot.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of an expressive speech act?
options: I warn you about the danger.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Expressives express psychological states (apologizing, thanking, congratulating).

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of a directive speech act?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Directives try to get the hearer to do something (requests, commands, suggestions).

ingliz_tili.text: What is a declarative speech act?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Declarations change reality (e.g., 'I now pronounce you husband and wife').

ingliz_tili.text: What is the illocutionary act?
options: The literal act of uttering words
The intended meaning or force of an utterance
The effect of an utterance on the hearer
The grammatical structure of a sentence
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Illocutionary act: what the speaker intends to do (e.g., warn, request, promise).

ingliz_tili.text: What is the perlocutionary act?
options: The literal act of uttering words
The intended meaning of an utterance
The effect of an utterance on the hearer
The grammatical form of a sentence
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Perlocutionary act: the actual effect (e.g., persuading, frightening, convincing).

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of a locutionary act?
options: Intending to request closing the window
Actually causing someone to close the window
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Locutionary act: the literal utterance of words with meaning.

ingliz_tili.text: What is the difference between direct and indirect speech acts?
options: Direct uses imperative, indirect uses declarative or interrogative
Direct is always polite, indirect is always rude
Direct uses past tense, indirect uses present
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Direct: 'Close the door.' Indirect: 'Could you close the door?'

ingliz_tili.text: What is common ground in pragmatics?
options: Shared knowledge between speaker and hearer
The physical location of the conversation
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Common ground is mutual knowledge, beliefs, and assumptions.

ingliz_tili.text: What is accommodation in pragmatics?
options: Changing one's speech to match the hearer
Adding new information to common ground without objection
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: Accommodation: when a speaker introduces a new presupposition, hearer accepts it.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of a presupposition trigger?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Stop' presupposes that he used to smoke.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a factive presupposition?
options: Presupposition triggered by factive verbs (know, regret, realize)
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'He knows that she left' presupposes 'She left'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which verb triggers a factive presupposition?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Regret' presupposes the complement is true.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a counterfactual presupposition?
options: If I had studied, I would have passed (implies I didn't study)
The sun rises in the east
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Counterfactual conditionals presuppose the antecedent is false.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a structural presupposition?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'When did she leave?' presupposes 'She left'.

ingliz_tili.text: What is the difference between presupposition and entailment?
options: Presupposition survives negation, entailment may not
Entailment survives negation, presupposition may not
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'He stopped smoking' negated → 'He didn't stop smoking' still presupposes he smoked.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a politeness strategy?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Politeness strategies minimize threats to face.

ingliz_tili.text: What is positive face?
options: The desire to be liked and approved of
The desire to be left alone
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Positive face: need for connection, appreciation, approval.

ingliz_tili.text: What is negative face?
options: The desire to be unimpeded and free
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Negative face: need for autonomy, freedom from imposition.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of off-record politeness?
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: Off-record: hints where hearer infers the intended meaning.

ingliz_tili.text: What is the face-saving act?
options: An act that protects or restores face
An act that is always rude
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Face-saving acts minimize threats to face.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a face-threatening act (FTA)?
options: An act that damages someone's public self-image
An act that is always polite
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: FTAs include criticism, requests, apologies, disagreements.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of bald on-record politeness?
options: I'm sorry to ask, but could you help me?
I wonder if you might help me.
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Bald on-record: direct imperative, no mitigation.

ingliz_tili.text: What is relevance theory?
options: Communication aims to be maximally relevant with minimal effort
Communication aims to be maximally polite
Communication aims to be grammatically correct
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: Sperber & Wilson's relevance theory: utterances are optimal relevance.

ingliz_tili.text: What is procedural meaning?
options: Meaning that guides how to interpret an utterance
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: Words like 'but', 'however', 'so' have procedural meaning.

ingliz_tili.text: What is conceptual meaning?
options: Meaning that encodes a concept (nouns, verbs, adjectives)
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'Cat', 'run', 'red' have conceptual meaning.

ingliz_tili.text: What is the difference between explicit and implicit communication?
options: Explicit is said directly, implicit is inferred
Explicit is polite, implicit is rude
Explicit is longer, implicit is shorter
Explicit is written, implicit is spoken
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Explicit: literal meaning. Implicit: implicature, presupposition.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a hedge?
options: A word or phrase that softens a claim
A word that strengthens a claim
A word that is always false
A word that is always true
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Hedges: 'sort of', 'kind of', 'I think', 'maybe'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of a hedge?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Sort of' softens the claim, reduces commitment.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a booster?
options: A word or phrase that strengthens a claim
A word that softens a claim
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Boosters: 'definitely', 'certainly', 'absolutely', 'without doubt'.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a discourse marker?
options: A word or phrase that organizes discourse
A word that is always formal
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Discourse markers: 'however', 'therefore', 'well', 'so', 'actually'.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of a discourse marker of contrast?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'However' signals contrast with previous statement.

ingliz_tili.text: What is repair in conversation?
options: Fixing a problem in speaking or understanding
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Repair includes self-correction, clarification requests, repetition.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a preference organization in conversation?
options: Preferred responses are more direct and socially expected
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: Preferred: agreement, acceptance. Dispreferred: disagreement, refusal (delayed).

ingliz_tili.text: What is an adjacency pair?
options: Two utterances that naturally go together (question-answer, greeting-greeting)
Two utterances that are always polite
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Adjacency pairs: question-answer, offer-acceptance, complaint-apology.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of an adjacency pair?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Question expects answer, greeting expects greeting.

ingliz_tili.text: What is turn-taking?
options: The organization of who speaks when in conversation
Always speaking at the same time
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Turn-taking involves transition-relevance places and overlap avoidance.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a transition-relevance place (TRP)?
options: A point where speaker change can occur
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: TRPs are points where current speaker can stop and next can start.

ingliz_tili.text: What is overlap in conversation?
options: Two or more speakers speaking at the same time
Speaking after the other has finished
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Overlap is generally brief and avoided in cooperative conversation.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a backchannel?
options: A listener response indicating attention (uh-huh, yeah, hmm)
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Backchannels show active listening without taking the turn.

ingliz_tili.text: Which is an example of a backchannel?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Uh-huh', 'mhm', 'yeah', 'right' are backchannels.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a conversational implicature?
options: Meaning inferred from violating or exploiting a maxim
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Conversational implicature arises from Gricean maxims.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a generalized conversational implicature?
options: Implicature that arises without special context
Implicature that depends entirely on context
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'A house' often implicates 'not my house' (generalized).

ingliz_tili.text: What is a particularized conversational implicature?
options: Implicature that depends on specific context
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: Particularized implicature requires shared knowledge of the situation.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a conventional implicature?
options: Implicature attached to specific words (but, therefore, even)
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: 'He is poor but happy' conventionally implicates contrast.

ingliz_tili.text: What is the difference between semantic meaning and pragmatic meaning?
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Semantics studies literal meaning, pragmatics studies meaning in context.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a speech act?
options: An utterance that performs an action
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Speech acts include requests, promises, apologies, warnings, declarations.

ingliz_tili.text: What is an indirect speech act?
options: An utterance where form and function don't match
An utterance that is always direct
An utterance that is always imperative
An utterance that is always interrogative
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Can you reach the salt?' (interrogative form, request function).

ingliz_tili.text: What is a direct speech act?
options: An utterance where form matches function
An utterance where form and function don't match
An utterance that is always polite
An utterance that is always rude
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'Pass the salt' (imperative form, command function).

ingliz_tili.text: What is a performative utterance?
options: An utterance that performs an action by being said
An utterance that describes a state of affairs
An utterance that asks a question
An utterance that is always false
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'I apologize' performs an apology; 'I promise' performs a promise.

ingliz_tili.text: What is a constative utterance?
options: An utterance that describes or states something (true/false)
An utterance that performs an action
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: 'The sky is blue' is constative (can be true or false).

ingliz_tili.text: What is the difference between explicit and implicit performatives?
options: Explicit uses performative verb, implicit does not
Explicit is longer, implicit is shorter
Explicit is formal, implicit is informal
Explicit is written, implicit is spoken
ingliz_tili.hint: C2: Explicit: 'I promise to come.' Implicit: 'I'll come.' (still a promise).

ingliz_tili.text: What is the preparatory condition for a promise?
options: The speaker can do the action and hearer wants it
The speaker cannot do the action
The hearer does not want the action
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Preparatory conditions ensure the speech act is appropriate.

ingliz_tili.text: What is the sincerity condition for an apology?
options: The speaker actually regrets the action
The speaker is happy about the action
ingliz_tili.hint: C1: Sincerity condition: speaker must have the appropriate psychological state.
